title: package-audit configuration
description: Input config for the READ-ONLY npm-package-usage auditor (PROMPT.md §1). Path precedence at runtime: --config <path> > CONFIG_PATH env var > ./config.json.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing at this schema. Ignored by the tool (excluded from config_hash).

githubHost.description: Bare host or host:port (port 1-65535; both bounds are enforced at runtime). Every gh call runs with GH_HOST=<githubHost>; API hostnames are never hand-built (GHES paths differ — gh handles them). null = use the default.

organizations.description: null/omitted = DISCOVERY MODE: enumerate every org the authenticated gh account is a MEMBER of and scan them all. An explicit array restricts scope (recommended for engagements). An explicit [] is 'configured' (not discovery): the effective list is then just the personal namespace when includePersonalNamespace is true, otherwise the run fails fast.

excludeOrganizations.description: Removed from the effective owner list in BOTH modes. null = use the default (none).

includePersonalNamespace.description: Opt-in: also scan the authenticated user's own repos (not an org, so off by default). null = use the default.

includeForks.description: Opt-in: include forked repos (excluded by default so a package's own forks don't double-count). null = use the default.

includeArchived.description: Opt-in: include archived repos. null = use the default.

maxReposPerOrg.description: Per-owner cap applied after sorting repos by pushed_at DESC. null = unlimited.

maxBranchesPerRepo.description: Per-repo cap on after-cutoff branches, newest-commit first. Branches past the cap are recorded as past-cap in the report and exports (visible in scope), but are not scanned for findings this run. The default branch is always scanned and never consumes a cap slot (the cap counts non-default branches only, so a repo can yield cap+1 scanned branches).

branches.description: Branch-name allowlist — exact name or Bun glob. null/omitted = unrestricted (every branch eligible, subject to cutoff/cap). [] = nothing but the default branch is eligible. A non-empty array restricts eligibility to matching branches. The DEFAULT branch is always eligible regardless of this list. A leading '!' is rejected at runtime (glob negation is not supported). excludeBranches is applied first (deny wins).

excludeBranches.description: Branch-name denylist — exact name or Bun glob. Matching branches are excluded from scanning (recorded as policy-excluded), EXCEPT the default branch, which is always eligible. Deny wins over 'branches'. null = use the default (none). A leading '!' is rejected at runtime.

excludeRepositories.description: Repository denylist — exact 'owner/repo' name or Bun glob over the full 'owner/repo' name. Matching repositories are dropped from every scan BEFORE archived/fork filtering and the maxReposPerOrg cap (a denied repo never consumes a cap slot). 'acme/*' drops one org; '*/legacy-*' drops a family across orgs; 'acme/legacy-api' drops one repo. '*' does NOT cross '/' (so ['*'] matches nothing) while '**' does (['**'] matches everything). Matching is CASE-INSENSITIVE (ASCII fold) — UNLIKE the case-sensitive 'excludeBranches', because GitHub repository identity is case-insensitive. Deny-only (there is no repository allowlist). A leading '!' or empty entry is rejected at runtime. There is NO dead-pattern warning for repositories (unlike excludeBranches) — confirm a pattern caught the repos you meant via the 'audit --plan' excluded list. null = use the default (none).

cutoffDate.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD; must be a real calendar date — enforced at runtime). Non-default branches whose last commit is strictly BEFORE this date are recorded as skipped-cutoff and never inspected; the default branch is always scanned regardless of this date.

concurrency.description: Fan-out widths + the global request cap (§5). All OPTIONAL: an omitted OR null block, and an omitted or null key, falls back to its default (organizations 3, repositories 8, branches 4). 'repositories' is the GLOBAL in-flight cap on gh/git/tar subprocesses — NOT a per-repo parallelism degree; it bounds real network work no matter how organizations×branches compose. 'organizations' sizes the owner fan-out pool; 'branches' the per-repo branch-unit pool. Excluded from config_hash (tuning speed never orphans resumable work). Each is capped at 64.

organizations.description: Max owners processed concurrently (default 3; null = default).

repositories.description: GLOBAL cap on in-flight gh/git/tar subprocesses (default 8; null = default) — the true network-politeness governor, not a repo-loop degree.

branches.description: Max branch-units scanned concurrently PER REPO (default 4; null = default). Unrelated to the root-level 'branches' allow/deny list, which shares only the name.

timeouts.description: Per-category spawn + liveness deadlines in SECONDS. Optional and per-field defaulted: override one knob (e.g. bulkApiSeconds on a slow VPN) and inherit the rest. null (or omitted) = use all defaults. Excluded from config_hash (raising a timeout never orphans resumable work).

controlApiSeconds.description: Deadline for quick control calls: gh auth/version/rate_limit/user, GraphQL, and repo/branch listings.

bulkApiSeconds.description: Deadline for raw content reads (file/blob/recursive-tree GETs) — larger than control so a big read on a slow-but-live link is not a deterministic failure.

cloneSeconds.description: Deadline for the hardened shallow git clone fallback.

heartbeatSeconds.description: Cadence of the liveness heartbeat emitted when the run is otherwise quiet (not a spawn deadline).

packages.description: The tracked npm packages. Names must be UNIQUE across the array (a name maps to exactly one registry per config).

name.description: The npm REGISTRY name — the single canonical identity for manifest/lockfile matching, API introspection, and reporting. A strict, fail-closed subset of npm names (uppercase allowed; exotic legacy punctuation and traversal/percent/query/fragment chars rejected) so the name can never reshape a registry URL (PROMPT.md §5.E); enforced identically at runtime. npm-alias installs are detected at scan time.

registryUrl.description: Registry BASE URL. Must be https:// and must not contain userinfo (user:pass@) — the pattern rejects an '@' in the authority; full URL validity (parseable, port <= 65535) is enforced at runtime. null = use the default.

registryAuthEnvVar.description: NAME of the env var holding a bearer token for a private registry. The var name participates in config_hash; the token value is never hashed or logged. Must be SET and non-empty at runtime.

excludeDirGlobs.description: Paths matching these globs are never scanned (vendored/generated code). null = use the default.

paths.description: Write locations. Both must resolve under ./data or ./output (write containment, PROMPT.md §0).

sqlitePath.description: SQLite database file (the durable source of truth).

outputDir.description: Where run-<run_id>.json and latest.json are written.
